summary: Critical spectral quality failure: >40% of spectra have S/N ratio below 5. Chimeric spectra rate 28% in DDA mode. Instrument calibration drift detected. All downstream identification findings from proteomics-reviewer must be considered suspect pending spectral quality remediation.

findings.title: Spectral quality critically degraded — 42% of spectra below S/N threshold
findings.message: QC report shows 42% of acquired spectra have signal-to-noise ratio below 5 (minimum acceptable threshold for confident peptide identification is S/N > 10). Contamination scan at acquisition time showed high background in 350-600 m/z range consistent with column bleed or plasticiser contamination. Chimeric spectra rate is 28%, meaning 28% of selected precursors contain co-eluting peptides at >10% relative intensity — these chimeric spectra produce false PSMs that pass database search scoring.
findings.impact: All downstream peptide and protein identifications from proteomics-reviewer are potentially unreliable. At 42% low-S/N spectra and 28% chimeric rate, the effective identification sensitivity is severely reduced and false positive rate is substantially elevated. PSM-level FDR calculated from this data does not reflect real identification quality because chimeric spectra systematically score well against incorrect peptide matches.
findings.recommendation: Re-acquire samples after: (1) cleaning the LC system (column replacement, solvent replacement), (2) checking for plasticiser contamination in sample preparation, (3) optimising AGC target and injection time for the sample complexity. Do not proceed to biological interpretation with current data quality.
findings.code_snippet:
# From QC report metrics
spectra_below_sn_threshold: 42.3%
chimeric_spectra_rate: 28.1%
median_sn_ratio: 4.2
calibration_mass_error_ppm: 8.7  # should be <5 ppm for Orbitrap

findings.title: Mass accuracy drift detected: 8.7 ppm median error vs 1-2 ppm expected
findings.message: Orbitrap mass accuracy should be < 5 ppm RMS after internal calibration. calibration_log.txt shows median mass error of 8.7 ppm at acquisition time, with drift from +3.2 ppm to +14.1 ppm across the 72-hour acquisition window. This drift suggests either a calibration lock mass was not used or the Automatic Gain Control was misconfigured.
findings.impact: At 8.7 ppm median error, database search with tight precursor tolerance (5 ppm) will miss a fraction of real PSMs. The search config uses 10 ppm tolerance which partially compensates, but increases false match rate due to wider window.
findings.recommendation: Enable real-time recalibration with a lock mass (e.g., m/z 445.12003 polysiloxane). Verify AGC target and reduce if source is AGC saturation. Recalibrate instrument before re-acquisition.
